title: How do I respond, and what do I do when Peter Lynch the Fixed Operations Manager at LDV Parramatta refuses to provide me…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
How do I respond, and what do I do when Peter Lynch the Fixed Operations Manager at LDV Parramatta refuses to provide me his general manager and directors details  because  I want to complain about him, bad customer service, etc, and that they want to charge me to repair and assess defects or faults with the vehicle which are not of acceptable quality fit for purpose and that it is unsafe.

{On Wed, 10 May 2023 at 08:53, Peter Lynch <peter@autoretailgroup.com.au> wrote:
Hi Rocco
 
After our conversation via phone this morning and how everything was explained.
 
There is no requirement for this to occur, as they will only defer to the information that has been provided to you, by me.
 
Happy to inspect the vehicle for the concerns, if there is a manufacture’s defect the diagnosis and inspection fee will not be applied, until this time
 
There is a $165.0 inspection and diagnosis fee. Updated booking day is now the 12/06
 
Regards
 Peter Lynch
Fixed Operations Manager
Parramatta Auto Centre LDV and MG
 
 
From: Rocco Verrelli <rlverrelli@gmail.com>
Sent: Wednesday, May 10, 2023 8:21 AM
To: Peter Lynch <peter@autoretailgroup.com.au>
Subject: Re: Request for Repairs of Ute Under Warranty - LDV T60. Vehicle Reg. ENL76J
 
Hi Peter,

Can you please provide me the names and emails of your company general manager and directors. 

Regards,
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> 


On Wed, 10 May 2023 at 07:39, Peter Lynch <peter@autoretailgroup.com.au> wrote:
As I am current involve in moving service department sites, so I am in and out of the office.
 
This is why all correspondence is easier Via email. as again the I said this you said that scenario is not able to be played out.
 
As per my first reply we can book the vehicle in for an inspection on the 09/06 now due to other booking, with the understanding that there is a $165.0 charge should no manufactures defect be found with the vehicle.
 
Regards
 <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>
Fixed Operations Manager
Parramatta Auto Centre LDV and MG

 
From: Rocco Verrelli <rlverrelli@gmail.com>
Sent: Tuesday, May 9, 2023 6:29 PM
To: Peter Lynch <peter@autoretailgroup.com.au>
Subject: Re: Request for Repairs of Ute Under Warranty - LDV T60. Vehicle Reg. ENL76J
 
Dear Peter 
 
I called your office early this afternoon but was told you were unavailable. I spoke to James, who advised me to email you. I am still waiting for a call back from yesterday, and I believe I deserve the courtesy of a phone call before anything else.
 
Regards
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>
0411 744 672

Subject: Re: Request for Repairs of Ute Under Warranty - LDV T60. Vehicle Reg. ENL76J

You would also be notified by email to call me back. As I have not received a callback, can you please make sure you call me before the end of the day. This subject matter is urgent and needs to be resolved.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Subject: Escalating Issue – Vehicle Repairs and Customer Service Concerns

Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I am disappointed in the lack of resolution and assistance regarding my vehicle's defects or faults, which are affecting its quality, fit for purpose, and safety. I understand that you have decided not to forward me the contact details of your general manager and directors, leaving me with few options to voice my concerns about your handling of this matter and overall customer service experience.

As a customer and vehicle owner, I believe I am entitled to better treatment and resolution for the problems with my vehicle. Therefore, I have decided to take the following actions to escalate this issue further:

1. I will contact the Consumer Affairs department in my state and lodge a complaint against LDV Parramatta, detailing my negative experience with your customer service, lack of assistance with warrantable repairs, and possible breaches of consumer rights.

2. I will seek advice from a lawyer specializing in consumer law to ensure my rights are protected and determine if any legal action is warranted.

3. I will research online platforms and social media sites to share my negative experience with LDV Parramatta, in hopes that it will alert potential customers to these issues and ultimately lead to improved customer service and resolution for all parties involved.

I am not seeking confrontation, but a fair and just resolution for the situation. I would appreciate your reconsideration of this matter with urgency.

Sincerely,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Subject: Escalating Issue – Vehicle Repairs and Unacceptable Customer Service

Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I must express my extreme dissatisfaction with your persistent refusal to assist me in resolving the critical defects and faults affecting the quality and safety of my vehicle. Your refusal to discuss this issue over the phone, lack of flexibility in providing assistance, and unwillingness to facilitate contact with higher management is utterly unacceptable. 

As a customer, I expect transparent communication, reasonable assistance, and a fair resolution for the problems with my vehicle. Your obstructive behavior has left me with no choice but to take more assertive and proactive measures to seek justice.

The following actions will be taken in response to your uncooperative conduct:

1. Immediately lodge a formal complaint against LDV Parramatta with the Consumer Affairs department in my state, outlining your inadequate customer service, refusal to address my vehicle's warranty-covered repairs, and apparent breach of consumer rights.

2. Consult a reputable lawyer specializing in consumer law to ensure my rights are appropriately protected and explore the possibility of legal action.

3. Share my appalling experience with LDV Parramatta on relevant online platforms and social media sites to inform potential customers of your obstructive behavior and lack of assistance.

Your consistent obstacles have left me no choice but to escalate this issue further. I urge you to take my concerns seriously and engage in a productive conversation on how to promptly resolve this matter.

Sincerely,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

Check the following email for grammar, ect. that I wish to send instead of the one you have suggested:
Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I must express my extreme dissatisfaction with your lack of flexibility in providing me assistance and your unwillingness to facilitate contact with higher management, which is utterly unacceptable.

As a customer, I have the right to escalate my concerns to the appropriate level of management. I expect transparent communication with all levels of management and reasonable assistance and in fixing the defects and faults of the vehicle which are regarded as not of acceptable quality, not fit for purpose, and the vehicle is unsafe.  

I strongly disagree with the $165.0 inspection and diagnosis fee which I will not pay. As per the Australian Consumer Law, I am entitled to have defects or faults with my vehicle repaired or assessed free of charge if they are not of acceptable quality or fit for purpose. I expect all warranty issues to be fixed immediately from the list I have provided. Those from the list which you regard as not covered under warranty, you can decide not to fix. But for each item not fixed to provide me with a detailed reason in writing as to why they have been regarded as non warranty issues, as I believe they all are. I believe my vehicle's issues as I have advised you pose a safety risk and should be treated as a matter of urgency under warranty.

I am notifying that I hold you and and LDV Parramatta liable, that if I am involved in any accident as a result of the vehicle defects which you are refusing to fix, which are covered under my vehicle’s warranty-covered repairs, as it is a breach of consumer rights. You may wish to notify your insurer.  
  
I urge you to take my concerns seriously and engage in a productive conversation to promptly resolve this matter. I also expect to be given the contact details of your company's general manager and directors, that you have denied to provide me. 

Regards
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

I received the following reply from Peter Lynch  to the email I sent; prepare an appropriate strong response:

{On Fri, 12 May 2023 at 16:24, Peter Lynch <peter@autoretailgroup.com.au> wrote:
Good Afternoon, Rocco

As per the conversation and the email sent before, the inspection fee is to cover the time of the technician to assess whether the listed items are a defect and the root cause.

If a manufacturers defect is found the fee is not charged to yourself. As the manufacturer covers this cost, they do not cover time to assess the vehicle should no defect be present or the root cause not be a manufacturing fault.

Until the assessment has been made, we are unable to submit the request for warranty to LDV Australia. As for your rights you are correct that an accepted warranty claim for a manufacturer defect are to be repaired at no cost to yourself, the assessment if it turns out to be a non-warrantable item, as I have seen in the past, items can be affected from outside influences including but not limited to aftermarket wiring , dash camera , usb with incorrect data, damage from pests, these circumstances are when it would be paid by yourself.

At no stage are you being denied an assessment of the vehicle, and if at any point you are unsatisfied with the assessment or wish to try for an earlier booking, your warranty is valid Australia wide at any Authorised LDV Australia service centre.
 
If you do not believe the vehicle is not safe to drive, you have the option of contacting roadside assistance (1800 709 232) to inspect the vehicle and have it towed to your nearest dealer.

If you chose to drive the vehicle you are accepting to drive it in the condition it is in.
 
The current date for booking to assess the vehicle is the 13/06/2023.
Regards
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>
Fixed Operations Manager
Parramatta Auto Centre LDV and MG
Unit 1/1 River Road, Parramatta
NSW 2150
02 99338100

turns.text:
Subject: Re: Escalating Issue – Vehicle Repairs and Unacceptable Customer Service - LDV T60- Vehicle Reg. ENL76J

Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

While I understand that the inspection fee is intended to cover the technician's time assessing whether a defect is present and the root cause, I maintain that it is unreasonable to charge me for assessing a vehicle under warranty when the defects clearly pertain to quality, purpose, and safety.

I have brought these issues to your attention in good faith, seeking a fair and prompt resolution. Our disagreement over the inspection fee should not prevent my vehicle from being assessed and warrantable items repaired. I must reiterate my dissatisfaction with your inflexibility and reluctance to involve higher management.

As a customer, I have rights under Australian Consumer Law, and your current approach seems to contradict those rights. Since you have not been cooperative in addressing my concerns, I will be left with no option but to seek help from other avenues, including lodging a formal complaint with Consumer Affairs and seeking legal advice on this issue.

I once again request prompt assessment and repair of my vehicle's issues without unreasonable fees. Furthermore, I still request contact details of your company's general manager and directors.

I would appreciate an objective, professional, and empathetic approach to resolving this matter.

Regards,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

turns.text:
Subject: Re: Escalating Issue – Vehicle Repairs and Unacceptable Customer Service - LDV T60- Vehicle Reg. ENL76J

Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I am disappointed that you persistently ignore my concerns and specific requests. By doing so, you are neglecting my consumer rights and obstructing the proper warranty requirement method in resolving these issues. Your approach indicates a blatant disregard for my legal rights as an Australian consumer and the warranty protocols in place.

In your response, you have reiterated the inspection fee explanation, which you previously explained to me on our phone call on 10/5/23. I have already expressed my disagreement with this fee and shared my decision on how I expect the issue to be handled. Nevertheless, you continue to ignore my concerns and provide excuses that avoid the real issue: denying me my consumer rights and failing to adhere to warranty requirement legislation.

Your statement that "if you do not believe the vehicle is not safe to drive, you have the option of contacting roadside assistance to inspect the vehicle and have it towed to your nearest dealer" demonstrates an avoidance of your legal responsibilities and liabilities. I have already informed you of the safety issues that arise when using the vehicle's inbuilt features for their intended purpose, which makes driving the car unsafe. By suggesting I don't use those features to render the vehicle safe to drive, you are in effect denying me the product I initially purchased, contradicting the intended use and purpose of the vehicle.

It is concerning that you have not addressed any of my specific concerns and requests directly, and I again request a detailed explanation for each item you consider non-warranty issues. Your refusal to provide the contact details of your company's general manager and directors further heightens my frustration.

I strongly urge you to deal with my concerns in accordance with consumer rights and warranty protocols rather than deflecting responsibility and avoiding my specific requests. I expect a more responsible and understanding approach towards resolving this matter, which has been causing me distress and worsening my anxieties, as previously mentioned.

Regards,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

Subject: Re: Escalating Issue – Vehicle Repairs and Unacceptable Customer Service - LDV T60- Vehicle Reg. ENL76J

Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I am extremely disappointed by your persistent disregard for my concerns, specific requests, and consumer rights. You have obstructed the proper warranty requirement method in resolving these issues, neglected my legal rights as an Australian consumer, and failed to adhere to warranty protocols and practices.

Your repeated explanations regarding the inspection fee were already communicated during our phone call on 10/5/23, and I have since informed you of my disagreement with this fee and how I expect the issue to be handled. Regardless, you continue to avoid responding to my specific concerns by providing excuses that neglect the real issue – denying me my consumer rights and failing to follow warranty requirement legislation.

You have persistently evaded my request for the contact information of your company's general manager and directors, which only exacerbates my frustration with your lack of transparency and unwillingness to resolve these matters efficiently.

Your statement suggesting me to contact roadside assistance if I believe the vehicle is unsafe to drive demonstrates a blatant avoidance of your legal responsibilities and liabilities. I have already informed you of the safety issues arising when using the vehicle's inbuilt features for the intended purpose, rendering the car unsafe to drive. By suggesting I refrain from using these features to maintain a safe driving experience, you are effectively denying me the product I initially purchased and contradicting the vehicle's intended use and purpose. 

It is very concerning that you have intentionally failed to address any of my specific concerns and requests directly. I again request a detailed explanation for each item on the list that you consider non-warranty issues. Your refusal to provide the contact details of your company's general manager and directors aggravates my discontent and heightens my frustration.

I am appalled by the lack of care and support you have demonstrated, worsening my anxieties and stress through your uncompassionate handling of this matter, which is clearly related to manufacturing issues and a vehicle unfit for its intended purpose. 

I strongly urge you to deal with my concerns in line with consumer rights and warranty protocols, rather than deflecting responsibility and dodging my specific requests. Now more than ever, I expect a responsible, understanding, and transparent approach towards resolving this matter that has been causing me significant distress.

Regards,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

You have persistently evaded my request for the contact information of your company’s general manager and directors, which only exacerbates my frustration with your lack of transparency and unwillingness to resolve these matters efficiently.
Your statement suggesting me to contact roadside assistance if I believe the vehicle is unsafe to drive demonstrates a blatant avoidance of your legal responsibilities and liabilities. I have already informed you of the safety issues arising when using the vehicle’s inbuilt features for the intended purpose, rendering the car unsafe to drive. By suggesting I refrain from using these features to maintain a safe driving experience, you are effectively denying me the product I initially purchased and contradicting the vehicle’s intended use and purpose.
It is very concerning that you have intentionally failed to address any of my specific concerns and requests directly. I again request a detailed explanation for each item on the list that you consider non-warranty issues. Your refusal to provide the contact details of your company’s general manager and directors aggravates my discontent and heightens my frustration.
I am appalled by the lack of care and support you have demonstrated, worsening my anxieties and stress through your uncompassionate handling of this matter, which is clearly related to manufacturing issues and a vehicle unfit for its intended purpose.
I strongly urge you to deal with my concerns in line with consumer rights and warranty protocols, rather than deflecting responsibility and dodging my specific requests. Now more than ever, I expect a responsible, understanding, and transparent approach towards resolving this matter that has been causing me significant distress.
Regards,
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>}

Subject: Re: Escalating Issue – Vehicle Repairs and Unacceptable Customer Service - LDV T60- Vehicle Reg. ENL76J

Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I am writing to express my extreme disappointment with your persistent disregard for my concerns, consumer rights, and the proper protocol for resolving warranty issues. You have consistently ignored my specific requests and failed to address the issues I have raised, refusing to reply to the concerns I have shared. Your actions are denying me my consumer rights and neglecting the proper warranty requirement method for dealing with these issues. You and LDV Parramatta are ignoring consumer rights protocols, and this behaviour is unacceptable.

In our phone call on 10/5/23, you explained the inspection fee would cover the time of the technician to assess whether the listed items are a defect and the root cause. However, as I have expressed in my previous emails, I completely disagree with this fee and have shared how I expect the issue to be handled. Despite this, you continue to avoid addressing my specific concerns and providing excuses that fail to abide by warranty requirement legislation.

You have persistently evaded my request for the contact information of your company's general manager and directors, heightening my frustration with your lack of transparency and reluctance to resolve these matters efficiently.

Regarding the vehicle's safety, I have already informed you of the safety issues arising when using its inbuilt features for their intended purpose. Your suggestion to contact roadside assistance if I believe the vehicle is unsafe to drive demonstrates a blatant avoidance of your legal responsibilities and liabilities. By implying that I should refrain from using these features to maintain a safe driving experience, you are effectively denying me the product I initially purchased and contradicting the vehicle's intended use and purpose.

It is very concerning that you have intentionally failed to address any of my specific concerns and requests directly. I again request a detailed explanation for each item on the list that you consider non-warranty issues. Your refusal to provide the contact details of your company's general manager and directors further aggravates my discontent and heightens my frustration.

I am appalled by the lack of care and support you have demonstrated, worsening my anxieties and stress through your uncompassionate handling of this matter. This issue is evidently related to a vehicle unfit for its intended purpose due to manufacturing problems.

I strongly urge you to deal with my concerns in line with consumer rights and warranty protocols, rather than deflecting responsibility and avoiding my specific requests. I expect a responsible, understanding, and transparent approach towards resolving this matter that has been causing me significant distress.

Regards,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>
